raw:
I'll start by inspecting the changes and understanding the codebase structure.
Let me inspect the actual diffs and the new untracked files.
Let me read the full files to understand the context properly.
Now let me read memory.ts, learn.ts in full, and the new test file.
Let me look at the existing test to compare structure, and check the host's setWidget contract and theme.fg.
Now let me verify the host contract for `setWidget`, `theme.fg`, and the UI types referenced.
The host contract isn't referenced elsewhere in the repo. Let me find the actual type definitions in the pi package.
The pi package isn't in node_modules. Let me locate it.
Let me read the full UI type definitions to verify the setWidget contract, widget caps, and theme.fg signature.
`setWidget` contract confirmed: `string[] | undefined` with placement option. Now let me verify `ctx.ui.theme` and the `fg()` signature.
`ctx.ui.theme` exists (line 174 of ExtensionUIContext). Now let me find the `Theme.fg()` signature and valid tokens.
`theme.fg` has signature `fg(color: ThemeColor, text: string): string` — it takes **two args and returns a string**, not a curried `fg(token) => (text) => string`. This is a critical discrepancy with the implementation. Let me verify against the runtime JS.
`theme.fg` is the critical thing to verify. The type says `fg(color: ThemeColor, text: string): string` — **two args, returns a string**. The implementation treats it as curried `fg(token)(text)`. Let me confirm the exact lines and trace the failure.
This is a critical finding. The real `theme.fg` signature is `fg(color, text): string` — a **two-argument method returning a string**, not a curried `fg(token) => (text) => string`. The implementation at index.ts:79-88 treats it as curried. Let me confirm both failure modes (currying + `this`-binding) with a simulation.
Confirmed a **critical runtime bug**. The real `Theme.fg` is `fg(color, text): string` (two args, returns string, uses `this.fgColors`). The code at index.ts:79 does `const fg = theme?.fg` then calls `fg("toolDiffAdded")` as if curried — which both (a) detaches `this` and (b) misuses the arity. Let me confirm how other extensions in this repo correctly call `theme.fg`.
Confirmed. Let me verify the precise line numbers and check the bound-`this` case too for completeness.
Confirmed the root cause. Let me verify the two call-site error-propagation paths and that `ctx: any` masks the type error.
I have enough to render a verdict. Let me confirm the exact call sites that swallow vs. propagate the failure.
I have enough to render a verdict. Let me confirm the exact swallow/propagate behavior at both call sites by viewing the agent_end try/catch.
I have what I need. Let me confirm the trailing-whitespace nit and the `ctx: any` typing that masked the bug.
The implementation has a critical defect. Let me confirm exact line numbers for the call sites before writing the verdict.
## Review Complete

I traced the widget wiring against the real pi host contract. There is a critical runtime defect that makes the core feature throw in the exact environment it targets.

### Root cause
The real host API is `Theme.fg(color, text): string` — a **two-argument method that returns a string** (verified at `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/dist/modes/interactive/theme/theme.d.ts:3` and `theme.js:249`). The task brief even describes it this way ("`ctx.ui.theme.fg(...)`", "throws on unknown tokens like `bg()` does"). Every other extension uses it correctly as `theme.fg("accent", text)` (e.g. `subagent/index.ts:700`, `AskUserQuestion/index.ts:326`).

`index.ts` instead treats `fg` as curried *and* detaches it from its `this`:

```ts
const fg = theme?.fg ?? ((token) => (text) => text);   // line 79: detaches `this`
const palette = { added: fg("toolDiffAdded"), ... };    // lines 84-87: one-arg call
```

In interactive mode (the only mode where `theme.fg` exists and widgets actually render), `theme?.fg` is defined, so the `??` fallback never fires. `fg("toolDiffAdded")` then calls the real method with `this === undefined`, which throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'fgColors')` **at palette-build time (line 84)** — before `renderDiffLines`/`setWidget` are ever reached. I reproduced both failure modes (detached `this` and the wrong arity) in isolation.

Impact at the two call sites:
- `agent_end` (line 143): the throw is swallowed by the surrounding `catch {}` → founder sees **nothing**. This is a regression: the old code always emitted a plain `notify()`.
- `registerCommand` (line 168): the `try/finally` has **no catch** → the throw propagates out of the command handler as an error. The `/continual-learning` command surfaces an error instead of the diff.

The test suite passes only because `diff_test.sh` exercises `diff.ts` with a hand-rolled fake palette and never touches the `theme.fg` wiring in `index.ts`. That wiring has zero coverage, and the fake palette accidentally encodes the wrong (curried) contract.

### Verdict

REVIEW: REQUEST-CHANGES

BLOCKING:
- `extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:79` — `const fg = theme?.fg ?? ...` detaches the method from its `Theme` receiver; calling it loses `this.fgColors` and throws. Bind/wrap instead.
- `extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:84-87` — palette built by calling `fg(token)` with a single argument, assuming a curried `fg(token) => (text) => string`. The real signature is `fg(token, text): string`. Build the palette as wrappers, e.g. `added: (s) => theme.fg("toolDiffAdded", s)` (with a plain identity fallback only when `theme?.fg` is absent for RPC/print). As written, the diff widget never renders in interactive mode: `agent_end` silently shows nothing (a regression from the prior `notify()`), and the `/continual-learning` command throws out of its handler.

NITS:
- `extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:82` — comment "ctx.ui.theme.fg(token) returns a function that colors the string" documents the incorrect curried contract; update it to the two-arg form once fixed.
- `extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:63,71` — typing the closures as `ctx: any` is what allowed this misuse to slip past typecheck; using the real `ExtensionContext`/UI type would have surfaced both the arity and `this` errors at build time.
- `extensions/continual-learning/index.ts:80` — trailing whitespace on the blank line.
